feed.title: new backbone code release with bitcoind python test framework integration

entry.title: new backbone code release with bitcoind python test framework integration
entry.summary:
The latest update from the developer community involves significant progress on a project known as backbone, which has now reached its 6th public release. This new version primarily focuses on enhancing the quality assurance and testing phases of development. A notable achievement in this update is the successful integration and adaptation of the bitcoind's python functional tests, despite differences in runtime architecture which now supports a multi-daemon process. The updated code branch for this project can be accessed at [bitcoinbackbone.org](http://bitcoinbackbone.org).
Further developments include modifications to the backbone-cli, enabling it to effectively control the main daemon during tests. Various approaches are being explored to optimize how CLI commands are dispatched internally across different daemons like blocks and addresses. However, the initial setup, command sending from the Python framework, and shutdown processes are reportedly functioning better than anticipated.
An important aspect of this update is the utilization of bitcoind’s extensive Python testing framework and test vectors, which are particularly useful for networking and peer-to-peer coverage. While there is still considerable work required to fine-tune these elements specifically for backbone, the current integration serves as a robust base for further development. Developers interested in exploring or contributing can build upon this foundation and test through a provided demo at `./functional-test/p2p_addrelay_connection.py`, though it has been tested only on Linux so far.
Additionally, enhancements are being made to the backbone-servers, and there are ongoing experiments involving cryptographic protocols from a previous initiative known as civkit. These efforts aim to establish a foundational structure that supports the growth of a real peer-to-peer bitcoin economy. The ongoing experiments also serve as a practical test (often referred to as "dogfooding") for evaluating the ergonomics of backbone interfaces. As the project marks its first year, the emphasis remains on continuous building and maintaining a growth mindset within the development community.
